question.text: Which of the following is NOT typically an area covered during due diligence of an issuer company?

answer.text: Capital structure and capital built-up history
Personal tax returns of minority shareholders

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Due diligence covers promoter/promoter group, capital structure, capital built-up, risk factors, management, business operations, financial statements, and litigations. Personal tax returns of minority shareholders are not part of standard DD — the focus is on promoters and key management personnel, not minority holders. **The Trap:** Since KYC of promoters/KMPs is required, candidates might extend this to all shareholders. However, DD focuses on those who control and manage the company, not passive minority investors. **Cross-Exam Context:** The scope of due diligence is tested in NISM Series X-B (Investment Banking) with greater depth on financial and legal DD areas. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When reviewing DD findings, pay special attention to litigation disclosures and risk factors — these often reveal issues that management presentations gloss over.
